Feed post

Courtney Powell reposted this

Christine Tsai

• 3rd+

Investing in brilliant founders around the world @ 500 Global

2h •

💫 Recap of Q1 2026 at 500 Global 💫

2026 was off to a roaring start. I’m excited to share the latest from Q1, plus a bit of catchup from Q4. Momentum building momentum.

📣 Signals from the 500 Portfolio

+ 🚗 SpotHero entered into an agreement to be acquired by Uber
🐟 Sakana AI raised $135M Series B at $2.65B valuation to build sustainable AI in Japan
+ 🤝 humans& raised $480M at $4.48B valuation to build human-centric AI
+ 👁️ Elorian AI raised $55M to build native multimodal reasoning models
+ 💹 9fin raised $170M at $1.3B valuation to transform debt markets
+ 🧬 BillionToOne closed $314M in their IPO (NASDAQ:BLLN)
+ 🔐 UpGuard raised $75M Series C to expand its AI cyber risk posture management
+ 🧑‍💻 Nas.com raised $27M Series A to empower the solopreneur economy
+ 🌏 CARSOME raised $30M to further power regional expansion
+ 🤖 Level3AI raised $13M seed to fuel R&D and APAC expansion for enterprise AI customer engagement agents
+ 🌐 Monnai raised $12M to build the global standard for identity & risk reasoning
+ 🏥 Avo raised $10M Series A to advance its AI clinical platform
+ 🐾 Snout raised $110M in debt and equity to defray vet bill costs
+ 🛡️ Verisoul raised $8.8M Series A to fight fraud, fake users, and bots
+ ⚙️ Cortex AI raised $6M to build the data engine that robots depend on to learn
+ ✈️ MightyFly raised $10M to scale autonomous hybrid eVTOL aircraft
+ 💡 Tradespace raised $15M Series A to build the new IP operating system
+ 🦔 Quill Meetings raised $6.5M seed to build its sovereign Chief of AI Staff agent
+ 🔧 Crafting raised $5.5M to build infrastructure for AI-driven engineering

🎶 Oh, What a (Super)Night

We hosted AI SuperNight events during #CES and NVIDIA GTC, as well as curated gatherings at NeurIPS and in the Bay Area. These are intimate gatherings exploring the SOTA of AI amongst world class researchers & technical leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, xAI, Physical Intelligence, Meta #SIL, top universities, and more. The magic of our community is the brilliance of the people and quality of the conversations, collectively shaping the future of AI. If that resonates, we'd love to hear from you. 👉 https://tally.so/r/3lP77k

🌍 Global to Hyperlocal Transformation

500 was shortlisted to deploy $250M into startups as part of Digital Morocco 2030. In Nairobi, we successfully completed sustainable innovation programs in partnership with UNDP Africa & the Shell Foundation. We announced our partnership with Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance and Grand Challenges Canada as part of the Gavi SUF. New batches kicked off in 500 MENA & 500 Eurasia with founders building AI-native cos in cybersecurity, healthcare, enterprise, fintech, insurtech, & more.

👏 A Promotion and a Warm Welcome

Mei Chel Tan was promoted to Global Managing Partner, Nadia Karkar joined as Managing Partner, Luca Bucken joined as Executive Director of Government Relations, and Atul Mehta joined our Board.

And that's a wrap! Back to building⚡🦾

Feed post

Based on your profile and activity

Marek Wawro

• 3rd+

AI-Driven CTO | AI/ML Strategist | FinTech Innovator | Google For Startups Mentor

2d •

I just had a fake recruiter try to get me to run malware on my machine.

Here's how the scam works - and how to turn it against them.

You get a message about an exciting role. Good money, remote, sounds legit. They move you to a video call and at some point ask you to clone and run a GitHub repository as a "technical task."

That repo is the payload. The moment you run it, you're compromised.

I recognized the pattern early. But instead of just ending the call, I kept going long enough to get the repo URL.

That's the move.

Once you have the link - without cloning or running anything - go to GitHub, open the repo, click the ··· menu, and hit "Report repository." Select malware. GitHub will review and take it down, protecting every developer who might stumble across it next.

Then report the recruiter's profile on whatever platform they used.

You don't need to be a victim. You can be the person who gets the link and kills the campaign.

Red flags that should make you suspicious:
→ Pressure to run something "during the call"
→ Repo created days ago with no real commit history
→ Company that doesn't check out online
→ Offer that's suspiciously above market

Stay sharp out there. Share this if it helps even one person avoid the trap.

#cybersecurity #developerlife #infosec #recruitment #scamalert

    10h •

    Personal Branding in 2020: - Spam trends and hacks - Low competition for attention  - Content was the only thing that mattered - Become visible and people assume authority - Have a great hook and you got great engagement Personal branding in 2026: - You actually have to say something insightful - Establish authority before visibility improves - Positioning matters before content can - High competition for attention - Hacks have been saturated In 2020, just showing up worked In 2026, everyone is showing up So how do you separate yourself? 1. Publish content with substance, often 2. Clear high authority positioning  3. Stack proof of credibility  4. Own a strong POV  5. Build your IP That’s your baseline. Want to go further? 6. Scale across your team  7. Speak on stage and podcasts 8. Distribute content across 2-5x channels 9. Create systems to convert connections → clients  10. Associate with leading people, publications, products This is from my experience building 350+ personal brands for execs and entrepreneurs, as well as building my own personal brand to over 60,000 followers and $3M in revenue.

    Feed post

    Based on your profile and activity

    Basia Kubicka

    • 2nd

    AI PM • AI Agents • Rapid Prototyping • Vibe coding

    View my newsletter

    4h •

    Everyone wants Claude to write better code.
    Nobody wants to configure Claude.

    The expectations are sky-high.
    Skills. Agents. MCPs. Hooks.

    But ask one simple question:
    "What's in your .claude folder?"

    Watch what happens.

    Not "do you use Claude Code."
    Not "did you make a CLAUDE.md once."

    What's actually configured?

    Most people don't have a Claude problem.
    They have a setup problem.

    → CLAUDE.md empty, or a 500-line dumping ground
    → Zero hooks, zero guardrails
    → Same prompt retyped every new session
    → No skills, no sub-agents, no MCP connected
    → One overloaded conversation doing everything

    Then they wonder why the output keeps getting things wrong.

    Here's what each piece actually does for you:

    CLAUDE.md → the project brief Claude reads every session.
    Without it, you retype "use TypeScript strict, tests first, don't touch the migrations folder" forever.

    settings.json → permissions and model choice.
    This is where you stop approving the same bash command 40 times a day.

    skills/ → modular playbooks Claude only loads when relevant.
    Stops one bloated CLAUDE.md from dragging context into every conversation.

    agents/ → sub-agents with one job each.
    Code reviewer. Security auditor. Each runs in its own context so your main thread stays clean.

    hooks/ → scripts that fire before or after tool calls.
    Auto-format. Block unsafe commands. The stuff you keep forgetting to run manually.

    .mcp.json → connections to GitHub, Slack, your database, your design tool.
    Claude stops being a chat window and starts being part of the stack.

    Configuring Claude is not an engineering problem.
    It never was.

    It's a prioritization problem dressed up as a technical one.

    It means someone has to do the unglamorous setup work first.

    Until that happens, every "AI-powered workflow" is just
    a fresh chat with the same instructions retyped.

    Claude doesn't fix a broken setup.
    It scales it.

    That's not a feature.
    That's a blast radius.

    When did you last actually configure Claude Code?


    ---
    PS. I'm running a FREE Workshop:
    How to Use Claude & Position Yourself to Get Inbound Job Offers (w/o ever sending resumes)
    Sign up here: https://lnkd.in/e6f944k7
    ---


    👋 I write about AI every day at 9 am ET. Follow me (Basia Kubicka) for more like this.

    View my newsletter

    5h •

    AI is in serious, serious trouble. There are 8 billion people on planet Earth. Half of them don't have internet access. That leaves 4 billion online. 1.5 billion are boomers who still call IT support to open a PDF. So only 2.5 billion working-age people with internet are left. Of which, 70% have never opened ChatGPT once. That leaves 1 billion total addressable market. 80% of them only use it to write breakup texts and birthday wishes. That leaves 200 million using it for "work." 4 out of 5 are too scared it'll take their job to actually learn it. That leaves 40 million curious enough to try. It is known that 87.5% use it once a month to ask what the weather is. That leaves 5 million actual users. 4,999,998 tried it once, got a weird answer, and gave up. That leaves just 2 people who actually know how to use AI. You and me. I'm already all-in.

    • 2nd

    Prof. at University of Zagreb, Faculty of Law, Civil Law Chair, Intellectual Property, Ph.D., president of Croatian Music Institute

    1w •

    Pravni fakultet Sveučilišta u Zagrebu ima novu dekanicu - treću ženu u 250 godina povijesti. Stupam na svoju novu dužnost 1. listopada 2026. godine. 👩‍🎓

    Zahvaljujem svim nastavnicima te predstavnicima suradnika, asistenata, studenata i nenastavnog osoblja koji su danas na Vijeću pristupili glasovanju a posebno onima koji su meni dali povjerenje. 🙏 To povjerenje obvezuje.

    Ističem svoj profesorski tim prodekana: Aleksandra Maganić, Dora Zgrabljić Rotar, Lucija Vejmelka i Marko Jurić.

    I sada još malo osobnog:
    Ništa od ovoga što postižem na različitim područjima djelovanja nije rezultat slučajnosti – sve je to rezultat dugogodišnjeg i predanog rada, truda, ulaganja. Na mojem putu uvijek me prate snažne osobe, velike podrške i uvijek uspijevam s vrhunskim timovima koji su uz mene. Pa tako i danas.

    Zahvaljujem svom timu prodekana koji su zajedno sa mnom iznijeli ovaj proces u svim aspektima. Bez njih ne bih uspjela. 🩷

    Zahvaljujem onim svojim kolegama profesorima koji su od početka ovog procesa bili nepokolebljivo uz mene. 🩷

    Zahvaljujem svojem mentoru i učitelju prof. dr. sc. Igoru Glihi 🩷, njegov je poticaj bio presudan da se uopće upustim u ovaj proces i prihvatim razmišljanje da bih bila dekanica.

    Zahvaljujem Nini Obuljen Korižinek 🩷, ovoga puta u ulozi prijateljice, od koje svaki dan ponovo i ponovo učim kako ustrajno i uspravno koračati naprijed - što god da letilo, treslo se, lupalo i padalo oko mene.

    Zahvaljujem timu vrhunskih profesionalaca čije elitne specifične usluge sam koristila u ovome procesu:
    Boris Trupcevic 🩷, ovoga puta u ulozi mog osobnog strateškog, operativnog, poslovnog i medijskog savjetnika;
    @nensi

    Nensi Frizl Zečević 🩷, koja me nosi, neponovljivo, autentično i s potpunim fokusom na moju osobnost;

    PBS centar sportske izvrsnosti 🩷, timu trenerica i trenera koji se brinu da moj zdravi i veseli duh počiva u dobro balansiranom i utreniranom tijelu, spremnom izdržati nemale mentalne i fizičke napore.

    Naposljetku, zahvaljujem svojem sinu Ivanu 💞, on je centralna točka moga svemira, i svojoj obitelji 💗.

    P.S. Naravno da ostajem predsjednica Hrvatskog glazbenog zavoda u punom kapacitetu i prioritetno – reorganizirat ću svoje druge obaveze i neke od njih otpustiti na neko vrijeme.

    Jan Lokaj

    • 3rd+

    Senior Sports Management Professional | Specializing in International Sports Consultancy

    2h •

    Central and Eastern Europe is still underrated I have spent most of my career connecting clubs with facilities across Central and Eastern Europe – and I still feel this region is undervalued in the professional camp market. While the industry talks about the same famous hubs, high-quality pitches, strong hotels and very serious football people are quietly doing excellent work here. You can find: UEFA-standard pitches within a short drive of international airports. Hotels that already host national teams and top-flight clubs. Environments with fewer distractions and more privacy. Value that allows clubs to invest more in staff and support. This is not about “cheap alternatives”. It is about smart options. For many clubs, the right Central or Eastern European camp can be exactly the balance they need between performance, focus and budget. My guess is that over the next few years, more decision-makers will start to see that too.

    5h •

    𝐌𝐨𝐬𝐭 𝐛𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐞𝐬 𝐅𝐚𝐢𝐥 𝐭𝐨 𝐬𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐞
    𝐛𝐞𝐜𝐚𝐮𝐬𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐲 𝐭𝐫𝐲 𝐭𝐨 𝐬𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐞 𝐛𝐞𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐲’𝐫𝐞 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐲.

    Scaling feels like the obvious next step.

    So founders add more, 
    more leads, more people, more tools. Automation etc...

    But underneath, things aren’t aligned.

    Leads sit in one place.

    Data sits somewhere else.

    Decisions happen separately.

    Everything exists.
    𝐁𝐮𝐭 𝐢𝐭 𝐝𝐨𝐞𝐬𝐧’𝐭 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤 𝐭𝐨𝐠𝐞𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫.

    So when you scale,

    Things don’t break.

    They just slow down.

    Decisions take longer.

    Follow-ups get missed.

    Opportunities slip.

    This isn’t a scaling problem.
    𝐈𝐭’𝐬 𝐚 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐛𝐥𝐞𝐦.

    If your systems don’t work in sync,

    You don’t really have a system.
    Just disconnected parts.

    Most founders don’t measure this.
    They assume.

    That’s exactly what the RevOps Readiness Assessment shows.

    Take this test and in just 5 Mins

    You will know your companies readiness to grow

    Comment 𝐑𝐎𝐑 and I’ll share the link.

    #RevOps #BusinessSystems #ScalableGrowth #RevenueOperations

    Feed post

    New comment in your group

    Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science & Robotics

    Dibya Jyoti Datta • 3rd+

    1w •

    Most people are watching intelligence.
    The real story is behaviour.

    And almost no one is ready for that shift.

    We’ve been sold a simple idea.
    Smarter models = better results = fewer jobs.

    That story breaks the moment you see agentic AI in action.

    Because now AI doesn’t just answer.
    It plans.
    It acts.
    It remembers.
    It retries.

    And suddenly… it behaves.

    Here’s the shift most people miss:

    → Models are getting better
    → But models are not the edge anymore
    → Systems are the edge

    Anyone can access strong models today.
    Few know how to manage what those models actually do over time.

    That’s where things get messy.

    Because once AI starts operating, new problems show up:
    1. It forgets context when you need it most
    2. It gives confident but wrong outputs
    3. It loops, retries, and burns cost silently

    Simple demo systems look perfect.
    Real systems break in quiet ways.

    That’s the gap.

    The winners won’t be the ones with the smartest model.
    They’ll be the ones who control behaviour.

    So what actually matters now?

    → Memory that improves outcomes over time

    → Feedback loops that correct mistakes fast

    → Guardrails that stop bad actions early

    → Cost control before scale kills margins

    → Clear failure handling when things go wrong

    This is not shiny work.
    But this is where trust is built.

    And trust is what turns AI into real leverage.

    So ask better questions:

    What happens when it fails?

    What happens when it forgets?

    What happens when it is confidently wrong?

    If you can answer that,
    you’re not just using AI anymore.

    You’re managing it.

    I share my learning journey here. Join me and let’s grow together.

    Enjoy this? Repost it to your network and follow Dibya Jyoti Datta for more

    1,980 reactions1,980

    22 comments22 comments

    •

    240 reposts240 reposts

    Prashanth Reddy Sripuram Verified Profile 3rd+Prashanth Reddy Sripuram • 3rd+

    Delivery Manager | Atlassian Cloud Migration & Integration | Jira, JSM, Confluence | Governance & AI-enabled Service Delivery

    1w

    Strong perspective on systems being the edge. But what’s often called “AI forgetting” is really context drift and unmanaged branching. The real leverage comes from knowing when to intervene — reframing prompts, resetting context, and applying human governance at the right moment. That’s less about using AI… and more about operating it effectively.

    Reaction button state: no reactionLike

    Muhammad Usama follows this Page

    Alibaba Cloud Global

    122,915 followers

    Promoted

    Can AI translate the tactile warmth of a child’s imagination? 🍭✨

    Meet 11-year-old Cyrus Tan Hoei Sam, a "Little Dreamer" from Malaysia Federation Of The Deaf (MFD). He imagined a house made of soft red clay waking up to a "candy day." Using Qwen 3.6-Plus and Wan 2.7, Alibaba Cloud transformed his sketch into a high-fidelity animation.

    ✨ Qwen translated the intricate details of his red-clay home.
    ✨ Wan animated the gumballs and marshmallow clouds with fluid consistency.

    This is the power of inclusive technology—ensuring no dream is too quiet to be heard. 🌍🚀

    🔗: https://lnkd.in/gjxYHQmU

    #AlibabaCloud #Qwen #Wan #BloomingSilentDreams #MFD #AIforGood #LittleDreamers

      Brand partnership • [JOIN MY CONTENT CREATION EVENT WEDNESDAY!] Young female founder? Join us in Journee Girls Club 💜 The community for exceptional girls building businesses & traveling the world | Creator of 💌 Business Besties

      10h •

      LOTS of people on LinkedIn will tell you WHAT to post... But almost none of them will actually do THIS ↴



      → Actually sit down and help you create content in real time!! 🥹

      I've been hearing a lot lately that even the most established Linkedin creators are getting SO bored with posting their usual content...

      👎🏼 While the ideas they’re actually excited about are collecting dust in their notes app

      & I get it - because some people on this platform make you believe that having fun and making sales are mutually exclusive 🙃

      It can be so tempting to go to all the "How to succeed on Linkedin" webinars too, whether you're new to the platform or an OG - I usually come out of those feeling FIRED UP too

      👉🏼 But the sitting down and actually DOING it??? another question 😬

      That's exactly why I'm bringing our most popular content creation event to LinkedIn in collaboration with Stanley!!!

      Stanley is the only Linkedin tool I genuinely LOVE using, legit better than ChatGPT or Claude at actually catching my VIBE and helping me ENJOY writing content!!! 😙💅🏻

      → Seriously just try it: https://lnkd.in/gnyM5k4i


      💜 He'll be our lab partner in Content Lab LIVE...

      ↳ The space where we actually CREATE content together!!!

      To be clear, this isn't another webinar where someone talks AT you for an hour

      This is a FUN, live online experience where you'll:

      🧪 Discover a new content hypothesis to test
      🧪 Experiment with ideas using prompts + hooks
      🧪 Learn how to use Stanley as your lab partner
      🧪 Co-work in the call so you actually get stuff DONE
      🧪 Get real-time peer feedback on what you create

      👉🏼 You'll leave with 10 ready-to-post content ideas

      (Not just loads of advice to action, actual ideas you can post THIS WEEK that are actually fun and have personality 🤪)

      I've been running Content Lab in Journee Girls Club every month and the girls consistently get their highest engagement, land clients and generate leads from the posts we create together 🥹

      I'm so excited to finally open it up to the public in collaboration with Stanley!!!

      📅 Wednesday 22nd April, 4pm UK / 10pm BKK

      → Register here: https://lnkd.in/gcPZ_geg

      (There might also be a fun surprise if you join live 👀)

      ——

      P.s. Stanley is the only tool that's helped me stay consistent & keep levelling up over the last year - I use it as a content coach to make ME a better writer, and he ALWAYS catches the vibe!! 💅🏻

      Feed post

      Ema Hasicevic

      • 1st

      Head of Business Development @Linkbound & Co-Founder @Dealion | 360° LinkedIn for B2B pipeline | Building First Revenue Club in the Western Balkans

      9h •

      We have officially stopped buying from logos. LinkedIn has pushed corporate profiles aside and replaced them with thought leadership from people. 64% of hidden decision makers trust an individual's expertise over company-generated content. This does not mean you stop using the company page entirely. You just change what it is for. It is a storefront and a transition point to your website, but it is no longer your distribution engine. Your distribution engine is your people. The DACH region has been running this for years. Employee advocacy programs across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland are already structured around this. The result is that they attract candidates and clients at the same time. When you amplify executive voices, you close the distance between your company and the buyer who needs to trust you before they ever say yes to a meeting. Research shows that just 3% of employees sharing content drives 30% of your total brand engagement. From a business development perspective, this is simple: 1. No visibility = no warm entry 2. No trust = longer sales cycles 3. No presence = always starting from zero Most companies compensate with more outbound, more pressure, more KPIs. The cost of that approach shows up in close rates, in sales cycles that drag for months, and in the reason the best message your team ever wrote got ignored by someone who had never heard of you. You will invest in one of these paths either way. You either build trust at scale, or you pay the price of being a stranger every time you hit send.

      Just two days left. Two open calls. One message: don't wait.

      Our co-founder and CGO Ismir Harambasic sat down with the Euronews BiH team, who visited us right here at our office, to talk about what we're building and why it matters.

      From our Internship Program for young talent to AI FORWARD, our EU & Germany co-funded initiative supporting micro, small, and medium businesses in BiH with free AI training - there's never been a better time to be part of the Bloomteq universe.

      But the clock is ticking. Both calls close in just two days, April 5th.
      Don't miss your chance to grow with us.

      Apply for Bloomteq internship with your CV and portfolio via careers@bloomteq.com

      Thank you to #Euronews for the visit, and for shining a light on what's happening in BiH, and for bringing this story to audiences across both Bosnia and Herzegovina and Serbia.

        Feed post

        Additi Upadhyay loves this

        Vivek Gautam

        • 2nd

        Founding Designer @ RevSure AI | 0→1 & Beyond | B2B SaaS (Enterprise) | Former Founding Designer @ Asper AI (acquired by Fractal Analytics) | First Designer @ Playo (B2C)

        7h •

        I had to take my “diamond” out on 14th February …yes, it was a kidney stone removal, not a romantic proposal! 💎

        I’ve been using Even Healthcare for the past 3 years and have always supported their vision as a startup making healthcare simpler and less stressful for people in India.

        What stood out for me from the beginning is the unlimited free consultations with doctors across so many specialties like physicians, cardiologists, gynaecologists, dermatologists, dietitians, you name it. If they suggest any tests, those get reimbursed smoothly, and if an online consult doesn’t feel right, I can go to any nearby doctor and get that fee reimbursed too.

        That flexibility helped a lot, but let’s be honest from a user’s point of view, the one thing that still felt missing was the comfort of meeting the doctor in person. Sometimes you just want them to see you properly, especially when something doesn’t feel straightforward.

        Last year, when Even opened their own hospital in Bengaluru, it finally bridged that gap.

        When the time came for the procedure, I chose Even Hospitals in Bengaluru. The process was surprisingly smooth - admission took just about 30 min, the doctor explained everything clearly, and a dedicated person handled all coordination. The nurses were experienced, professional, and caring throughout. Even on Sunday discharge, everything including consumables was settled cashless without any hassle.
        Post-discharge follow-ups with clear recovery guidance were thoughtful and reassuring.

        Overall, my experience with both their online + reimbursement services and the actual hospital services has been excellent. A big thank you to the doctors, nurses, and the entire Even team for building this with so much care and heart. As someone who roots for Indian startups solving real problems, I’m genuinely happy to see them growing and putting patients first.

        Highly recommended! And yes, I’m now sipping more water like my life depends on it. 🫠 🧉

        Additi Upadhyay and 16 others reactedAdditi Upadhyay and 16 others
